questions.question: What is the fundamental execution difference between Vue's `setup()` and React's component function?
options: Vue's `setup()` runs on every render; React's component function runs once per instance
Vue's `setup()` runs once per instance; React's component function runs on every render
Both run once per instance, but Vue's runs asynchronously
Both run on every render, but Vue batches the DOM updates

questions.question: Why are stale closures not a problem in Vue's Composition API?
options: Vue clones all state values before each render to ensure freshness
Vue uses Proxy objects so `ref.value` always reads the current reactive value
Vue's `watch` automatically re-creates closures when dependencies change
Vue compiles templates to avoid closures entirely

questions.question: Which React pattern is NOT needed in Vue because `setup()` only runs once?
options: Passing props from parent to child components
Wrapping functions in `useCallback` to prevent child re-renders
Subscribing to external stores with a custom hook
